name.description: Your display name — the page heading and the browser title. 1–50 characters.

vpa.description: Your UPI ID, like name@okaxis. Copy it from your UPI app — this is where money goes. Never edit it by hand.

tagline.description: A one-line tagline shown under your name.

avatar.description: Path to a square avatar in public/, e.g. /avatar.png. Omit for an initials disc.

bio.description: A short bio. Supports **bold**, _italics_ and [links](https://…). Up to 500 characters.

label.description: The platform name, e.g. "GitHub" — also the icon and the link’s accessible name.

url.description: The full https:// URL to your profile on that platform.

socials.description: Up to 6 social links, shown as icons under your name.

creator.description: Who you are — name, UPI ID, bio, social links.

description.description: What the project is. Line breaks are kept. Up to 500 characters.

url.description: Where the project lives — repo, site, or write-up.

works.description: Your projects, up to 12. Omit or leave empty to hide the section.

chai.description: Pricing and the donor’s amount + message controls.

presets.description: The one-tap chips: 1–4 named tiers, each a label and a rupee amount. Sorted by amount automatically.

label.description: What the chip says — e.g. “Cutting chai”. Keep it short; it sits above the price.

amount.description: What this chip pays, in whole rupees (e.g. 20).

emoji.description: Optional glyph shown above the label — e.g. “☕” or “☕☕🍟”. 3 max, decorative only.

allowCustomAmount.description: Show the “enter your own amount” field. Default true.

maxAmountWarning.description: Amounts above this show a “double-check” caution — a nudge, never a block.

defaultNote.description: The payment note used when a donor leaves the message field empty. ≤ 60 characters; plain words are safest.

allowDonorMessage.description: Let donors attach a one-line message to the payment. Default true.

theme.description: Palette mode and your accent colour.

mode.description: light / dark pin the palette; auto follows the visitor’s OS. Default auto.

accent.description: Your accent colour (hex / rgb() / oklch()). The one palette knob — recolours the CTA, borders and links, never the off-white canvas.

provider.description: The analytics provider. Only "posthog" in v0.

apiKey.description: Leave this out — the key is injected at build from the VITE_POSTHOG_KEY environment variable, never written here.

host.description: Your PostHog ingestion host. Default is EU cloud; use the US host if you signed up there.

analytics.description: Optional analytics. Omit this whole block to ship zero tracking code (the default).

meta.description: Page title, description, social card, and language.

title.description: Browser tab and social-card title. Defaults to “Buy {your name} a chai”.

description.description: Meta description for search results and social cards.

ogImage.description: The picture shown when your link is shared. Leave it out and /og.png is used whenever public/og.png exists — replace that file to use your own, 1200x630.

language.description: The page’s BCP-47 language tag (sets <html lang>). Default "en".

branding.description: The buy-me-a-chai template’s own links. Defaults to the template author’s; override to make them yours.

maker.description: The template author — their name and support page.

name.description: The template author’s display name, shown in the footer “Support …” link.

supportUrl.description: The author’s own support page — Buy Me a Coffee, Ko-fi, GitHub Sponsors, …

project.description: The template repository — its name, repo URL and template URL.

name.description: The template repo / package name — the “Powered by …” credit in the footer.

repoUrl.description: The canonical template repository, linked from the footer credit.

templateUrl.description: GitHub’s “Use this template” URL — where the masthead CTA sends a visitor who wants their own page.
